generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LEPIDIUM. Lepidium Bonariense, D. C. Natural order , Cruciferæ. Preparation , Tincture or trituration of the leaves. Authority. Mure, five provings, Pathogénésie Brésilienne. MIND. ► Emotional. ► She fancies herself alone in a graveyard, pursued by a ghost, and screams so that her voice is lost next day; yet nobody hears her (fourth day), [2] . ► Merry mood (curative effect, after the sadness of the previous days), (fourth day), [2] . ► Merry mood; she laughs about everything (sixth day), [1] . ► Sadness on awaking (second day), [1] . ► Sad, uneasy, quarrelsome, dissatisfied; with deep sleep (fifth day), [1] . ► Deep melancholy; with thoughts of sickness (fourth day), [1] . ► Intellectual. ► Want of ideas; inability to think; with indifference to everything (first day), [3] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo, with disposition to fall forward (fourth day), [1] . ► Vertigo, with falling forward of the head, on setting down a basin of water (third day), [2] . [10.] ► Vertigo and nausea, in the evening (eighth day), [1] . ► Dizziness, at about 9. A.M.; her head fell forward, and she imagined the floor was sinking under her; everything seemed turning around (second day), [1] . ► General Head. ► Heaviness of the head, with an indefinite feeling of uneasiness in the brain, especially on the left side (third day), [5] . ► Headache, with heat at the forepart of the vertex (first day), [3] . ► Violent headache, at 4 P.M. (first day), [3] . ► Violent headache in the morning, lasting till 2 P.M. (thirteenth day); violent headache in the evening; with sense of compression in the forehead, from temple to temple (sixteenth day); the violent headache continues, and is felt all day; beginning regularly at about 10 A.M., and ceasing at night (seventeenth, eighteenth, and nineteenth days), [5] . ► Constrictive pain in the head (fourth day), [1] . ► Constant dull headache (first day), [3] . ► Pressing and pricking pain around the head (fifth day), [2] . ► Pain on rising, as from the pressure of a heavy ring on the head (third day), [1] . [20.] ► On waking, acute pains in the head and posterior cervical muscles (fourth day), [5] . ► Beating and throbbing in the head, from within outward, which causes it to hang down (fourth day), [2] . ► Violent headache, as if the brain were beaten with a hammer from the inside and jumped up and down in an empty space; it lasts half an hour (second day), [5] . ► Forehead. ► Pain in the forehead, with throbbing in the left side, for ten minutes (sixth day), [1] . ► Headache above the orbits and in the temples, worse by contact and when raising the eyes (third day), [2] . ► Tightness in the forehead, as if compressed, at 11 o'clock, lasting a quarter of an hour (first day), [3] . ► Pulling in the forehead and at the root of the nose (first day), [3] . ► Throbbing over the forehead, making the head fall forward; it does not last long (fourth day), [2] . ► Vertex. ► Boring pain on the left side of the vertex at noon; it pierces to the ear (third day), [1] . ► Parietals. ► Pain passing quickly through the right side of the head, from the top of the left parietal bone to the eyebrow (sixth day), [1] . [30.] ► Pains in the whole left side of the brain, commencing above the eye (which feels contracted), and spreading to the vertex, the back part of the head, and lastly to the nape of the neck, where they remain seated for some time; they appear in succession, one never being felt until another has ceased, and seem to be in the membranes (second day), [5] . ► Slight lancinations in the head, always on the left side (twenty-fourth day), [5] . ► External Head. ► Sensitiveness of the scalp (fifth day), [4] . EYE. ► Eyes red, injected, and painful (seventh day), [5] . ► Burning sensation in the eyes, at 11 o'clock (fifth day), [4] . ► Pain in the right eye, as from a heavy ring which presses against it and causes an itching in the inner canthus (fifth day), [2] . ► Lancinations in the right eye, without redness, for three hours (third day), [3] . ► Pricking in the eyes, in the evening, as from something very astringent (sixth day), [5] . ► Orbit. ► Lancinating pains above the eyes, and in the temples (seventh day), [5] . ► Lancinations above the left eye, and in the left temple (twenty-first day), [5] . ► Lids. [40.] ► Eyelids very weary; she can scarcely lift them all night (second day), [3] . ► Weight on the eyelids, with inclination to close them (first day), [3] . ► Lachrymal Apparatus. ► Painless lachrymation (third day), [2] . ► The left eye is full of water; the pain is worse when moving; it follows the eyebrow, and lasts six hours; left eye still watery, worse in the open air; ending with itching; for ten minutes (sixth day), [1] . ► When attempting to read, her eyes fill with tears (fourth day), [1] . ► Ball. ► Eyeball and lids much injected (sixth day), [5] . ► Vision. ► Dimness of vision, as if a white bandage were over the eyes; when she looks up, the sky seems gray (sixth day), [1] . EAR. ► External. ► Pain around the right ear, as if pricked by needles, relieved by rubbing; it lasts a quarter of an hour (third day), [1] . ► Beating pain above the left ear (second day), [1] . ► Middle. ► The left ear feels as if completely stopped up; hearing is lost on that side (sixth day), [2] . [50.] ► Lancinations in the ears (sixth day), [1] . ► Pricking in the ear and down along the jaw (third day), [1] . ► Great itching in the right ear, worse when stooping, lasting half an hour (second day), [1] . ► Hearing. ► Hard of hearing (fourth day), [1] . ► Roaring in the left ear (third day), [2] . ► Roaring in the right ear, for almost two minutes, at 5 o'clock (seventh day), [4] . NOSE. ► Objective. ► Nose swollen on the left side, with pain, which is less in the open air, and worse by contact (third day), [2] . ► Violent coryza, with inflammation and itching of the nose (third day), [2] . ► Epistaxis from the right nostril; the blood is black and clotted, with itching; the epistaxis continued until 1 o'clock, when it became profuse; the blood, however, being light red (fifth day), [2] . ► Subjective. ► Heat in the nose, and sensation as from a current of cold air in the left nasal fossa (third day), [1] . [60.] ► Burning in the corner of the left nostril, with pricking at the tip of the nose, for fifteen minutes (sixth day), [1] . ► Great itching in the right nostril (third day), [1] . FACE. ► A streak of pain from the temple to the chin, as if the face were cut with a razor (sixth day), [2] . ► Cheeks. ► Pain in the left cheek-bone, with redness (third day), [1] . ► Pain in the right cheek, which passes off by pressing on the malar bone (third day), [2] . ► Painful but short-lasting sensation in the right zygoma (third day), [5] . ► Pricking on the cheeks (fourth day), [1] . ► Chin. ► Pain in the left lower jaw, extending towards the shoulder; for five minutes (eighth day), [1] . ► Pain as from a cut by a penknife on the top of the jaw, short-lasting (sixth day), [2] . ► Lancination in the left jaw-bone (twenty-first day), [3] . [70.] ► Pain and beating in the left jaw, for ten minutes (eighth day), [1] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► Pain in the teeth of the left lower jaw, felt also in the ear, and causing deafness for three minutes (seventh day), [1] . ► Pains in the jaw-bones, as if all the teeth were affected (twentieth day), [5] . ► Sharp pain in the right lower jaw-bone, in which all the teeth are quite sound (second day), [5] . ► Toothache on the right side; at 1 o'clock (sixth day), [1] . ► Toothache; the teeth are soft and on edge, all day; every day since taking; not at night (seventh day), [1] . ► Gums. ► Acute pain in the lower gums lasting an hour (second day), [1] . ► Tongue. ► Burning at the tip of the tongue as if from spice, with an expansive feeling in the papillæ (third day), [2] . ► Itching on the tongue, like pricking, for five minutes (eighth day), [1] . ► General Mouth. ► Great dryness of the mouth and throat (sixteenth day), [5] . [80.] ► Heat in the left side of the mouth, as from eating spice (third day), [1] . THROAT. ► Burning in the throat, with nausea, and noise in the ears when swallowing saliva (fourth day), [1] . ► Drawing pain deep in the throat; tongue thick, as if much swollen (first day), [2] . ► Swallowing. ► When eating a cake, it sticks in the sophagus, and then suddenly falls as if shaken down; the same occurs when drinking (third day), [2] . ► External Throat. ► Pain from the left ear to the lower part of the neck, along the course of the carotid artery, worse when inclining the head to the right (sixth day), [2] . ► Pain like a cramp in the right side of the neck, extending to various parts of the shoulder and arm; relieved by pressure (third day), [2] . ► Violent drawing pain from the shoulder to the ear, hindering the motion of the head for three minutes (seventh day), [1] . ► Feeling as if a string were pulled from the shoulder to the ear (seventh day), [1] . ► Lancination from the ear to the shoulder (fourth day), [2] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Hunger after dinner (second day), [1] . [90.] ► Longing for chocolate, salad, green fruit (third day), [1] . ► Longing for fruit, with loathing of all food (fourth day), [2] . ► Longing for water cresses, but on seeing them it is replaced by aversion (third day), [2] . ► No appetite (twenty-second and twenty-third days), [5] . ► Complete loss of appetite; loathing of meat (twenty-first day), [5] . ► Loathing of food, especially meat (twentieth day), [5] . ► Aversion to meat (sixth day), [2] . ► Aversion to milk (first day), [3] . ► Thirst. ► Thirst (first day), [3] . ► Thirst; dry mouth (sixth day), [1] . [100.] ► Great desire for vinegar (third day), [1] . ► Longing for tea (sixth day), [1] . ► Intense longing for coffee (first day), [3] . ► Eructation. ► Eructations (twentieth and twenty-fourth days), [5] . ► Eructations of air, without acidity, in the evening (seventeenth, eighteenth, and nineteenth days), [5] . ► Foul eructations all day (seventh day), [4] . ► Sour eructations (fourth day), [4] . ► Nausea. ► Nausea all night (fourth day), [1] . ► Nausea after dinner, when holding the head down (sixth day), [1] . ► Nausea, which keeps getting worse, and reaches its height in half an hour; it goes off on stirring about (third day), [2] . [110.] ► Great nausea, for half an hour (seventh day), [2] . ► Qualmishness before eating (fourth day), [3] . ► Qualmishness followed by nausea before eating (third day), [3] . ► Stomach. ► Difficult digestion; weight on the stomach (sixth day), [1] . ► Weak feeling in the stomach, and tendency to collapse (third day), [2] . ► Faint feeling in the stomach, from 4 to 7 P. M. (fifth day), [4] . ► Pain at the pit of the stomach after eating, worse from contact, when walking (third day), [1] . ► Pain at the pit of the stomach, which is felt in the left breast, and jumps about; pricking in both these places at once (eighth day), [1] . ► Distressing pain at the pit of the stomach, with nausea; at 11 o'clock (fourth day), [4] . ► Burning in the stomach, with feeling of dryness and irritation (first day), [3] . [120.] ► Coldness in the gastric region, which spreads all over the chest as far as the throat (third day), [2] . ► Compression, as by a girdle around the waist, apparently caused by a contraction of the diaphragm; it was especially felt at the epigastrium (third day), [2] . ► Dull pain at the stomach, followed by great desire to vomit (second day), [1] . ► Stomachache; nausea before eating (first day), [3] . ► Acute pain in the pit of the stomach after eating (second day), [2] . ► Sensation as if a knife were cutting into the epigastrium (fourth day), [2] . ► Pain in the cardia as if cut with a penknife; short-lasting (sixth day), [1] . ► Pain in the pit of the stomach, as if the body were cut in two by a bar; it lasts half an hour (third day), [2] . ► Beating at the pit of the stomach, with pricking, worse when drawing breath; short-lasting (sixth day), [2] . ► Palpitation at the epigastrium (fourth day), [1] . ABDOMEN. [130.] ► Pricking, as from pins, in the left hypochondrium (third day), [1] . ► Colic in the umbilical region (fourth day), [1] . ► Nightly pain in the whole abdominal region (seventh day), [4] . ► Dull pain in the abdomen, chiefly on the right side, not aggravated by pressure (first day); at 4 o'clock (second day), [3] . ► Stitch in the left side of the abdomen; at 2 o'clock (fifth day), [4] . ► Colic caused by damp air (third day), [2] . ► Severe colic, better when sitting, with emission of flatulence; at 8 P. M. (fourth day), [4] . ► Great sensitiveness of the abdomen, as from an internal wound; at 7 A. M. (fifth day), [4] . ► Worm-colic in the hypogastrium for five minutes, with ineffectual urging to stool, and tenesmus (third day), [2] . STOOL. ► Diarrha (fourth day), [4] . URINARY ORGANS. [140.] ► Heaviness and pressure on the bladder, when urinating (fourth day), [1] . ► Urine clear white, and not very thick (seventh day), [1] . ► Dark urine (third day), [2] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► The blood which is discharged on the second day of menstruation is darker and coagulated (third day), [1] . ► The menses cease after twenty-four hours (fourth day), [1] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Nocturnal cough; light sleep (eighth day), [1] . ► Cough with hoarseness (eighth day), [1] . ► Dry cough, with loss of breath (fourth day), [2] . ► Dry short cough, which causes desire to spit, and after repeated efforts produces a salty saliva (eighth day), [1] . ► Thick, tenacious expectoration, with roaring in the ears (fourth day), [1] . [150.] ► Thick, salty sputa, raised with difficulty (sixth day), [1] . ► Slight hæmoptysis after the cough (fourth day), [1] . ► Respiration. ► Short breathing (fifth day), [4] . ► Suffocative fit at night, while lying (seventh day), [4] . ► Suffocative oppression, after eating (third day), [1] . CHEST. ► Pain in the left lung, striking through the back, and worse when carrying anything heavy; for twelve hours (seventh day), [1] . ► Pain in the pectoralis major, commencing under the axilla, and a few minutes extending to the cardiac region (third day), [2] . ► Contraction of the diaphragm, as on the day before (fourth day), [1] . ► Violent lancinations under the right third rib, for some minutes, every now and then (first day), [5] . ► Stitch in the right lung for five minutes (third day), [1] . ► Sides. [160.] ► Pain in the left side, which takes away the breath (seventh day), [2] . ► Pain in the right side, as from a tight girdle, as if the diaphragm were contracted; it hinders breathing, and is aggravated by pressure (third day), [1] . ► Stitch in the left side, at 8 o'clock; it disappeared spontaneously (first day), [3] . ► Stitch in the left side; when drawing breath, the pain seems to penetrate the body like a knife-stab, and in a little while coalesces with the previous pain; three times (seventh day), [1] . ► Mammæ. ► Sensation as from a cord around the right breast; painless (seventh day), [1] . ► Cutting as with a penknife below the left breast (fourth day), [1] . ► Lancination in the right breast, less when standing up (third day), [1] . ► Pain with palpitation of the muscles under the right breast, which diminishes as it extends to the axilla (third day), [2] . ► Itching of the nipples; congestion of the mammary glands; hardness of the breasts; the itching does not last long (eighth day), [1] . HEART. ► Præcordium. ► Constrictive pain at the heart, extending to the left axilla (seventh day), [2] . [170.] ► Severe piercing pains in the region of the heart, with lancinations under the false ribs (second day), [5] . ► Sensation as if a knife were slowly piercing the heart relieved by pressing hard upon the part for a few minutes; renewed in the evening (third day), [1] . ► Heart's Action. ► Spasmodic tremor of the heart (third day), [1] . ► Palpitation of the heart (second and twenty-third days), [5] ; at 3 o' clock (seventh day), [4] . ► Palpitation of the heart, felt in the side, with a severe pain which takes away the breath; worse when stooping, relieved by lying down (third day), [1] . ► Transient palpitations (twentieth day), [5] . ► Violent palpitation of the heart (seventeenth, eighteenth, and nineteenth days), [5] . NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Pain in the right side of the nape of the neck, relieved by moving the neck and rubbing it (third day), [1] . ► Severe and repeated pains in the muscles of the neck and of the left shoulder-blade (thirteenth and sixteenth days); in morning (fifteenth day), [5] . ► Back. ► Crampy pain behind the neck for five minutes (sixth day), [2] . [180.] ► Pain in the back as from a nail, extending to the interscapular region (seventh day), [2] . ► Sensation as from a violent blow across the middle of the back (fourth day), [2] . ► Throbbing in the back, which seems to proceed from the aorta (first day), [3] . ► Dorsal. ► Pains for five minutes from the shoulder to the middle of the back, with lancinations which take away the breath (sixth day), [1] . ► Rheumatic pain in the right shoulder-blade, lasting only a few minutes (third day), [2] . ► Pain in the shoulder-blade, as if a needle were piercing the bone; for three minutes (eighth day), [1] . ► Lancination in the right shoulder-blade (sixth day), [5] . ► Lancination, as from a knife, in the spine of the scapula, shifting to the other shoulder; less when standing erect (third day), [1] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► The limbs feel bruised (third day), [2] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Left arm very numb; pain in left shoulder as if struck with a hammer (second day), [2] . [190.] ► Pain in the left arm, she can scarcely lift it; short-lasting (third day), [3] . ► Severe pain in the left arm before rising; she cannot stretch it; the more she covers it the greater is the pain; at last she uncovers it, and the pain ceases at once (first day), [1] . ► Paralytic pain in the left arm, when held still for some time (third day), [4] . ► Pain in the right arm, as if struck by a hammer, with numbness (third day), [2] . ► Shoulder. ► Pain in the shoulder, which extends around the neck, compresses it like a snake, and pricks her in the pit of the stomach and nape of the neck (fifth day), [2] . ► Feeling at 2 o'clock as if a small hammer were beating quickly on the right shoulder, for nearly a minute (fifth day), [4] ► Acute pain under the left axilla (third day), [2] . ► Acute pain in a crescentic space under the axilla (third day), [1] . ► Pain as from a cut with a penknife under the left axilla, at 6 o'clock (second day), [4] . ► Lancinations under the axilla (fourth day), [1] . [200.] ► Pricking under the axilla (third day), [1] . ► Arm. ► Lancinations from the elbow to the shoulder-blade (fourth day), [1] . ► Pain as if from a cut with a penknife, with itching over the right biceps (eighth day), [1] . ► Hand. ► Crampy pain in the left hand (fifth day), [2] . ► Crampy pain in the right hand, which prevents closing it; followed by horripilation all over (second day), [2] . ► Intense lancinating pains in the muscles and phalangeal articulations of the right hand; two hours later, similar sensations in the right shoulder-blade, with pains in the right wrist; all these pains are short-lasting but well-marked (fourth day), [5] . ► Fingers. ► Pain in the bone of the left thumb, with trembling when attempting to use it (fourth day), [1] . ► Pain, while running, in the middle finger, which remained stretched for four minutes (eighth day), [1] . ► Rheumatic pain and stiffness of the left index finger, which remains stretched for some minutes (third day), [2] . ► Crampy pain at the right ring finger, extending to the elbow and contracting the flexor communis digitorum, with redness at the root of the nail; it lasts twenty minutes, and passes off in the open air (sixth day), [1] . [210.] ► Pricking in the tip of the middle finger, drawing it up so that it cannot be stretched out (seventh day), [2] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Great weariness in the legs for three hours (fourth day), [3] . ► Hip. ► Pain in the left hip, extending to the knee, with weakness of the leg, which abates when sitting down (eighth day), [1] . ► Bruised pain in the right hip, relieved by pressure (third day), [2] . ► Thigh. ► Pain in the gluteus maximus, for one hour at 6. 30 o'clock (sixth day), [4] . ► Pain below the loins in the left gluteus maximus, as if contracted and twisted; at two o'clock (second day), [4] . ► Pulling along the course of the left sartorius muscle (second day), [4] . ► Knee. ► Contraction of the legs, which cannot be bent on account of the stiffness of the bend of the knee (third day), [2] . ► Pain in the bend of the knee, as if a tendon were drawn slowly inwards (third day), [2] . ► Pricking pain in the knee, relieved by walking (fourth day), [1] . ► Leg. [220.] ► Severe pain in the anterior portion of the right tibia (first day), [5] . ► Toes. ► Sensation as if the left great toe were struck by a hammer (fourth day), [2] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Great weariness (third day), [2] . ► General physical and moral prostration, loss of appetite, and aversion to food (fourth day), [4] . ► The open air feels very cool (first day), [3] . ► Almost constant physical and mental torpor (first day), [3] . ► Heaviness of the body (second day), [5] . ► Inclination to walk about (first day), [3] . ► Pain in the trunk from above downwards at 10 A. M. (second day), [4] . ► Violent pains in the muscles of the neck, thigh, and left leg, very transient, but recurring frequently in the course of the day (seventeenth, eighteenth, and nineteenth days), [5] . [230.] ► Transient lancinations in the abdomen, sides, and breasts (fourth day), [5] . ► Pain as if scratched by a cat, lasting an hour, and followed by heat of the feet (second day), [1] . SKIN. ► Pricking of the skin, followed by itching (third day), [1] . ► Pricking at the temple, with itching, which becomes general and lasts all day (eighth day), [1] . ► Pricking in the left side of the face (first day), [3] . ► Pricking between the breasts (third day), [1] . ► Pricking at the shoulder-blade, extending to the right side of the neck (third day), [1] . ► Formication above the left shoulder (fourth day), [1] . ► Itching of the whole under surface of the chin, from ear to ear; it proceeds from the throat (eighth day), [1] . ► Great itching in the right corner of the lips, at 7 A. M. (third day), [2] . [240.] ► Great itching on the backs of the hands (third day), [2] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► Constant inclination to yawn (fourth day), [4] . ► Frequent yawning (first day), [3] ; (third day), [2] . ► Sleepiness from noon till 3 P. M. (third day), [2] . ► Drowsy all day (second day), [5] . ► Heavy and drowsy, unable to keep her attention on her work; at 3 P. M. could apply herself without difficulty (fifteenth day), [5] . ► Great drowsiness, with horripilation in the legs, as before an attack of fever; very transient (first day), [3] . ► Comatose drowsiness, with inability to sleep (first day), [3] ► Sleeps all the time (third day), [5] ► Deep sleep (sixth day), [2] [250.] ► Heavy sleep, with great numbness on waking; feeling as if bruised all over (first day), [2] ► Sleeplessness. ► Sleeplessness at night; general lassitude, as on the third night (sixth day), [1] ► No sleep at night; general restlessness followed by pricking all over, all night (second day); same restlessness as on the day before, followed by similar sleepiness (third day), [3] ► Very restless all night, without apparent cause; no sleep (fifth day), [5] ► No sleep until midnight (third day), [2] ► Slept well until midnight; no sleep after that until daylight; pains in the body when she stirred (first day), [1] . ► Very bad night; great nervous restlessness (second day), [5] . ► Short sleep (fourth day), [1] . ► Dreams. ► Sad dreams, with great fear on waking; the sadness and fear continue for a quarter of an hour (eighth day), [1] . ► Dreams at night about the dead (third day), [4] . [260.] ► At night, dreams of talking with dead people; great restlessness (second day), [1] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. ► General chilliness, as in the beginning of a fever (seventh day), [2] . ► Coldness, as when in convulsions (second day), [1] . ► When the air strikes her, it causes rigors and cold sweat (third day), [2] . ► Rigor, with paleness and circles around the eyes; this febrile attack abates by covering herself, and getting into a profuse perspiration; it is succeeded by malaise (seventh day), [1] . ► Heat. ► Heat after the coldness, especially in the loins (seventh day), [2] . ► General heat, with dull pain; left side most painful (first day), [1] . ► Great internal heat and restlessness after lying down; she has to get up again (first day), [3] . ► Heat in the head, without sweat, at 6 o'clock (fourth day), [4] . ► Heat in the head, with cold sweat and a little fever, at 2 o'clock (fourth day), [4] . [270.] ► Heat in the left side of the face, at 11 A. M. (second day), [1] . ► Great heat in the back, of short duration, followed by rigors (first and fourth days), [3] . ► Very hot face, in the evening (second day), [1] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Evening ), Vertigo, etc.; pricking in eyes. ► ( Night ), While lying, suffocative fit. ► ( After dinner ), When holding the head down, nausea. ► ( Pressure ), Pain in side. ► ( Stooping ), Itching in ear; palpitation of heart. ► Amelioration. ► ( Night ), Headache. ► ( Lying down ), Palpitation of heart. ► ( Pressure ), Pain in hip. ► ( Sitting ), Colic. ► ( Standing erect ), Lancinations in scapula. ► ( Walking ), Pains in knee.
